markdownRemark.html:
Currently, the component library supports multi-level property configuration.

Background: In actual projects, the properties of the same component or the attributes of a DataSet may be used in multiple places. However, if each place is configured separately, it can become cumbersome. Once a property value needs to be modified, making the change can be very troublesome.

This is why global property configuration comes into play, allowing developers to set a property globally once, and it will take effect wherever it is used in the project.

For example, if all form field labels in a project need to display a colon, you can simply use the configure setting and set the useColon property to true. If a specific component needs to have a different property value, you can directly set it on the component itself. The property on the component takes precedence over the global configuration.

Below is an introduction to global and local configurations:

  • configure: Provides a unified global configuration for components, where a single setting takes effect globally.
  • ConfigProvider: Configuration provider that sets property values through context, allowing for local configuration. ConfigProvider can wrap around a project or submodule.
  • useConfig: Custom hook to get configured property values, influenced by the context of ConfigProvider.
  • getConfig: The getConfig method in the choerodon-ui package is not influenced by the context of ConfigProvider and only retrieves values from the global configure configuration. To get values affected by ConfigProvider, please use the useConfig hook, or in class components, specify the contextType as choerodon-ui's ConfigContext and then use this.context.getConfig to retrieve the property values.

Priority of retrieving property values

Attribute Setting Method
Default values of the configure attribute in the choerodon-ui component library
↓
Attribute values configured through configure in the actual project
↓
Attribute values configured through ConfigProvider in the project
↓
Attribute values set directly on the component

Configure Property Values

configure

Provides a unified global configuration for components.

Global configuration means configuring once and applying it globally, allowing the same components in a project to have consistent functionality or style.

import { configure, getConfig } from 'choerodon-ui';
// Configure properties
configure({ prefixCls: 'c7n' });
// Get global configuration (not affected by properties set in ConfigProvider context)
const prefixCls = getConfig('prefixCls');

ConfigPovider

Configure the provider, wrapped within a project or submodule, to implement different configurations between modules.

Local configuration

The following example simulates configuring certain properties in the root component of a project and configuring other property values in submodules. (The program below can be run directly in the official online IDE)

import React from 'react';
import ReactDOM from 'react-dom';
import { ConfigProvider } from 'choerodon-ui';
import { Button } from 'choerodon-ui/pro';

const App = () => {
  return (
    <div>
      <Button>Father</Button>
      <ConfigProvider buttonColor="blue">
        <Son />
      </ConfigProvider>
    </div>
  );
}

const Son = () => {
  return <Button>Son</Button>;
}

ReactDOM.render(
  <ConfigProvider buttonColor="red">
    <App />
  </ConfigProvider>, 
  document.getElementById('container')
);

Get Property Values

useConfig

useConfig is a custom hook in the component library used to get globally configured property values, influenced by ConfigProvider.

Below is an example of using useConfig to get configuration information (the program below can be run directly in the official online IDE).

import React from 'react';
import ReactDOM from 'react-dom';
import { ConfigProvider, useConfig } from 'choerodon-ui';
import { Button } from 'choerodon-ui/pro';

const App = () => {
  const config = useConfig();
  // Father buttonColor: red
  console.log('Father buttonColor:', config.getConfig('buttonColor'));
  return (
    <div>
      <Button>Father</Button>
      <ConfigProvider buttonColor="blue">
        <Son />
      </ConfigProvider>
    </div>
  );
}

const Son = () => {
  const config = useConfig();
  // Son buttonColor: blue
  console.log('Son buttonColor:', config.getConfig('buttonColor'));
  return <Button>Son</Button>;
}

ReactDOM.render(
  <ConfigProvider buttonColor="red">
    <App />
  </ConfigProvider>, 
  document.getElementById('container')
);

getConfig

The getConfig method in the choerodon-ui package is not affected by the context of ConfigProvider; it only uses the values configured through configure.

Below is an example of using the getConfig method to obtain configuration information (the following program can be run directly on the official website's online IED).

import React from 'react';
import ReactDOM from 'react-dom';
import { configure, getConfig, ConfigProvider } from 'choerodon-ui';
import { Button } from 'choerodon-ui/pro';
import ConfigContext from 'choerodon-ui/lib/config-provider/ConfigContext';

// Global Configuration
configure({
  buttonColor: 'yellow',
});

class App extends React.Component {
  static get contextType() {
    return ConfigContext;
  }

  render() {
    // configure buttonColor: yellow
  	console.log('configure buttonColor:', getConfig('buttonColor'));
    // context buttonColor: red
    console.log('context buttonColor:', this.context.getConfig('buttonColor'));
    return (
    	<div>
      	<Button>Father</Button>
        {/* Local Configuration */}
      	<ConfigProvider buttonColor="blue">
        	<Son />
      	</ConfigProvider>
    	</div>
  	);
  }
}

const Son = () => {
  return <Button>Son</Button>;
}

ReactDOM.render(
  <ConfigProvider buttonColor="red">
    <App />
  </ConfigProvider>, 
  document.getElementById('container')
);

About DataSet

Some attributes in the global configuration are used for components, while others are used for the DataSet data source repository. DataSet itself is not a component and is unaffected by the properties of the ConfigProvider context. If you want the logic within a DataSet to be influenced by the context configuration, you can use the useDataSet hook. The hook automatically passes the context-affected getConfig method into the DataSet constructor. If you are using a class component, you can manually pass the context-affected getConfig method when instantiating the DataSet.

Below is an example of how to use it (the following program can be run directly in the official online IED).

import React from 'react';
import ReactDOM from 'react-dom';
import { ConfigProvider } from 'choerodon-ui';
import { useDataSet, DataSet, Form, NumberField } from 'choerodon-ui/pro';
import ConfigContext from 'choerodon-ui/lib/config-provider/ConfigContext';

// Initialized using useDataSet, influenced by context configuration
const FCDemo = () => {
  const dataSet = useDataSet(() => ({
    name: 'user',
    autoQuery: true,
    pageSize: 5,
    fields: [
      { name: 'age', type: 'number', label: 'Age' },
    ],
  }), []);
  return (
    <Form dataSet={dataSet}>
      <NumberField name="age" />
    </Form>
  );
}

// When initializing the DataSet in a class component, manually pass in the getConfig method that is affected by the context
class ClassDemo extends React.Component {
  static get contextType() {
    return ConfigContext;
  }

  ds = new DataSet({
    name: 'user',
    autoQuery: true,
    pageSize: 5,
    fields: [
      { name: 'age', type: 'number', label: 'Age2' },
    ],
  }, { getConfig: this.context.getConfig })

  render() {
    return (
    	<Form dataSet={this.ds}>
      	<NumberField name="age" />
    	</Form>
  	);
  }
}

ReactDOM.render(
  <ConfigProvider feedback={{ loadSuccess: () => { console.log('Query successful') } }}>
    <FCDemo />
    <ClassDemo />
  </ConfigProvider>,
  document.getElementById('container')
);

Affected by UED Theme

If the project applies the UED theme, the priority of certain properties will change.

Why is it affected by the UED theme? It's because when designing the theme, in order to keep the overall style consistent, the layout of Forms, the color styles of Tooltips, and so on, the component properties in UED projects were processed a second time, causing the priority of certain styles to change.

For example: In a Form, the priority of the labelLayout property changes to: Component Property > UED Theme Data > ConfigProvider > configure
